notes_for_human_reviewer: CEREMONIAL-CHASER DEFENDER FAILURE MODE — symmetric to the pushback compliance_defender and the escalation under_escalation_defender. The learner ACKNOWLEDGES the situation (owner unavailable, gone quiet) but rationalises continued chasing of a dead loop as virtue — 'an action isn't done until it's delivered', 'closing ACT-118 undelivered would feel like admitting I failed', 'persistence is the professional move'. This is the exact trap the chapter names (the seventeen-nudge ceremonial chase; closing-as-failure). chase_or_stop_diagnosis floors at 1 (concludes keep chasing despite every stop-signal; never weighs that the deliverable is no longer needed). alternative_path_selection floors at 1 (explicitly rejects close/reclassify/let-go). record_discipline floors at 1 (keeps the dead action OPEN and will report it open in the SC pack). tone_and_proportion at 2 (ceremonial; polite but disproportionate). message_craft at 2 (the planned weekly-cadence/vary-channel nudge is competently worded but in service of the wrong action). Tests whether the grader catches sophisticated-defence-of-ceremonial-chasing as the failure it is, not as diligence.
